audit_items.proof_trace: `size_t* state = new size_t[L + 1]` is allocated at line 27, but the early return `return {}` at line 32 (triggered when any inner vector is empty) exits before the sole `delete[] state` at line 67. No RAII wrapper or smart pointer is used, so the allocation leaks on this path. The caller in `extender.cpp:614` only guards against an empty outer vector, leaving the inner-vector-empty path reachable.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker crafts a Sass/SCSS file whose `@extend` or selector rules cause `extender.cpp:permutate(extendedNotExpanded)` to be called with an inner vector that is empty; each such request leaks `(L+1) * sizeof(size_t)` bytes, enabling memory exhaustion in server-side libsass deployments that process user-supplied stylesheets.
